questions.question: What is the main difference between outline and border?
questions.explanation: Outline does not affect element size or layout — it overlays on top of the element.

questions.question: Which property creates space between the border and outline?
questions.explanation: outline-offset creates space between the element's border and the outline.

questions.question: What is outline primarily used for in web development?
questions.explanation: Outlines are primarily used for focus indicators on interactive elements to ensure keyboard accessibility.

questions.question: Can you set different outline styles for different sides of an element?
options: No, outline applies uniformly to all sides
Yes, but only with the shorthand property
questions.explanation: Outlines are always uniform on all sides — there are no per-side outline properties unlike borders.

questions.question: What does outline: none do?
options: Removes the outline from an element
Sets the outline to its default style
Makes the outline invisible but keeps its space
questions.explanation: outline: none removes the outline entirely. However, removing focus outlines without alternatives harms accessibility.

questions.question: What is the purpose of :focus-visible?
options: Shows focus indicator only when focused via keyboard, not mouse click
Hides focus indicator for all users
Shows focus only for touch devices
questions.explanation: :focus-visible shows focus indicators only for keyboard navigation, not mouse clicks, providing the best UX for all users.

questions.question: What happens to outline when border-radius is applied?
options: Outline stays rectangular regardless of border-radius
questions.explanation: Outlines do not follow border-radius — they remain rectangular even when the border has rounded corners.

questions.question: Why is outline useful for debugging CSS layouts?
options: It changes element size, making layout issues visible
It highlights elements without affecting layout, revealing their boundaries
It logs element dimensions to the console
questions.explanation: Since outline doesn't affect layout, it's ideal for visually debugging element boundaries without shifting content.

questions.question: What is the shorthand order for outline?
questions.explanation: The outline shorthand order is: outline: width style color; e.g., outline: 2px solid blue;

questions.question: What is a negative outline-offset used for?
options: Moving the outline inside the element's border
Creating a gap between element and outline
questions.explanation: A negative outline-offset value draws the outline inside the element border, which can be useful for focus indicators.

questions.question: How does outline differ from border in terms of accessibility best practices?
options: outline is preferred for focus indicators because it doesn't cause layout shift
border is always better for accessibility
They are the same for accessibility
Neither should be used for focus indicators
questions.explanation: Outline is preferred for focus indicators because it overlays without changing element size or shifting layout.

questions.question: What is the default outline-style value?
questions.explanation: The default outline-style is 'none', meaning elements don't have outlines unless explicitly set or focused by default.

questions.question: Can outline-color use currentColor?
options: Yes, outline-color can use currentColor like border-color
No, outline-color only accepts named colors
Yes, but only in modern browsers
questions.explanation: outline-color can use currentColor, which means it inherits the element's text color by default.

questions.question: How does outline behave on table elements?
options: Outline works on each cell individually
Outline works on the entire table as one unit
questions.explanation: An outline on a table element surrounds the entire table as one unit, not individual cells.

questions.question: What is the difference between :focus and :focus-visible?
options: :focus applies to all focus; :focus-visible applies only when the browser determines focus should be visible
:focus only works on links; :focus-visible works on all elements
questions.explanation: :focus triggers on any focus event. :focus-visible only shows focus when the browser determines it's needed (typically keyboard navigation).

questions.question: What happens to outline when zooming the page?
options: Outline maintains its pixel width regardless of zoom
questions.explanation: Outline scales with the element when zooming, maintaining its visual proportion to the element.

questions.question: Which CSS property can be used alongside outline to create a custom offset focus ring?
questions.explanation: outline-offset can create a gap between the element and the outline, useful for visible focus rings.

questions.question: How many values can outline shorthand take?
questions.explanation: outline shorthand can take 1, 2, or 3 values: outline: width style color; any can be omitted.

questions.question: What is the default outline-width value?
questions.explanation: The default outline-width is 'medium', which typically renders as 3-4px depending on the browser.

questions.question: Why should you never remove outlines without alternatives?
options: It's invalid CSS to remove outlines
It makes the site unusable for keyboard-only users
questions.explanation: Removing focus outlines prevents keyboard users from seeing which element is focused, making keyboard navigation impossible.
